title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: The technician is repairing my laptop right now.
questions.explanation: Tests the causative 'have' structure in the present continuous.

questions.firstSentence: ‘Don't go near the water,’ the lifeguard told the children.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs (warn) with negative infinitives.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sorry I didn't take your advice about the job.
questions.explanation: Tests 'wish' + past perfect to express past regret.

questions.firstSentence: It’s possible that they forgot about the meeting.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect (might have + past participle) for past possibility.

questions.firstSentence: I had never seen such a spectacular sunset before I went to Africa.
questions.afterGap: such a spectacular sunset before I went to Africa.

questions.firstSentence: Driving a car is not the same as riding a motorbike.
questions.explanation: Tests adjective phrases and prepositional use (different from).
